economic_notes: Economic Development segment includes motel, business centre, and fuel sales. COVID-19 impacted capital projects including new police detachment and water treatment plant upgrade. Community access restricted to prevent virus transmission. Capital projects put on hold as contractors were sent home.
notes: Audit is qualified due to absence of budgeted information (not a going concern qualification). Fiscal year is 2020 not 2024 as stated in the document. Housing and Infrastructure expenses combined into one segment ($4,998,820). COVID-19 note discloses significant disruption to capital projects. Large deferred revenue balance of $7.6M mainly from ISC First Nations Water & Wastewater Action Plan ($5.6M) and police detachment ($143K). Significant increase in cash from $5.8M to $12.5M driven by Water Treatment Plant operating account ($9.4M) and holdback accounts. Assets under construction of $11.9M (water treatment plant, police detachment). No schedule of remuneration included in the statements. Current liabilities calculated as accounts payable ($5,269,878) plus due to government ($626,083) = $5,895,961. Prior year ISC revenue shown as $7,028,014 in Note 15 but $12,267,549 federal transfers on income statement includes other federal sources.
